title: carex muricata subsp. contigua
intro: Carex muricata subsp. contigua, also known as the coastal hair sedge or rough sedge, is a sub-species of the Carex muricata plant, a type of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Carex muricata subsp. contigua include its ability to soothe coughs and colds, reduce fever, and treat eczema and other skin irritations. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a valuable resource in natural and alternative medicine.
